page: Hetzner Cloud cx23 is an entry-level virtual server built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, with 2 physical cores and a 1-thread-per-core configuration. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA. The instance is provisioned with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB per core, and includes 40 GB of local SSD storage. No dedicated GPUs are included in this configuration.

Performance benchmarks place the cx23 in the lower tiers of cloud hardware. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests register scores of 1028.88 and 2016.78 respectively, both falling within the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is similarly constrained, with read-only large block transfers reaching 20.91 GB/sec and write operations at 16.93 GB/sec. For lightweight database operations, the server handles Redis SET operations at 2.52 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput is measured at 1.77 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest hardware profile, the cx23 offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for non-demanding workloads. The trade-off for this low-cost resource density is limited compute and memory bandwidth. It is best suited for basic utility tasks, lightweight web serving, development environments, small-scale testing, or simple background cron jobs that do not require sustained high-performance CPU or memory throughput.
description: Hetzner Cloud cx23 is an entry-level virtual server utilizing the x86_64 architecture with 2 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of RAM and 40 GB of local SSD storage, without any GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate modest performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core scores in the bottom 10% of tested instances, and a Redis SET throughput of 2.52 million operations per second. Due to its shared CPU allocation and lower memory bandwidth, this instance is highly cost-efficient for lightweight, non-production workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, and low-demand background tasks.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server with shared compute resources and local SSD storage for lightweight utility workloads and development environments.
